floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: judge's respelling for the (*new_var)*0 alias is proven in-repo (InitHiraRmd_80041AC8 gets the same lh+lhu dual read with direct spelling); the a3++/a3--/t1++/t1-- pairs emit NO tar

frontier.hypothesis: Entry respelling per the in-repo sibling (`t0 = (u16)a2[0]; if (a2[0] == -2) goto end;`, new_var deleted) reproduces the s:5-6 lh+lhu pair byte-identically.
frontier.mechanism: cse.c keeps sign_extend-MEM and zero_extend-MEM as distinct exprs -> two loads survive (proven by InitHiraRmd_80041AC8's byte-match).
frontier.next_probe: sandbox func_8003FECC --disable all on the respelled variant (inc/dec pairs untouched); expect 0. This is the highest-confidence half of the cleanup.

frontier.hypothesis: The four inc/dec statements are inert -- deleting :465-468 leaves the sandbox at 0 because a3/t1's natural refs already win their temp-reg seats.
frontier.mechanism: flow.c cancels the paired +1/-1 before RA (or combine folds them); reg_n_refs delta only matters when an allocation is contested, and no callee-save contest exists here.
frontier.next_probe: sandbox a variant with only :465-468 deleted; if 0, delete. If score rises, diff localizes the contested reg -- then sweep natural forms before considering a FAKE-annotated redundant-arithmetic close (pending the filed ruling_question).

frontier.hypothesis: The full-clean form (both fixes together) also holds 0 -- the two constructs do not interact.
frontier.mechanism: the alias affects only entry-block CSE; the inc/dec pairs affect only loop RA weights -- disjoint pass surfaces.
frontier.next_probe: tools/sweep_variants.py with the 2x2 variant grid (alias-fix x incdec-removal) to confirm independence in one call, then verify-oracle on the winning full-clean form.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Two dead-operation cheats found: (1) `a3++; a3--;` and `t1++; t1--;` inside the loop — net zero effect, GCC-internal allocator steering; (2) `new_var = &a2[0]; if (a2[(*new_var) * 0] == (-2))` — the multiply-by-zero through a pointer alias is always 0 and has no semantic purpose beyond defeating CSE between the unsigned and signed reads of a2[0]. Clean re-implementation should: eliminate the dead increment-decrement pairs (they generate no target asm — DCE removes them), and replace the `(*new_var)*0` alias trick with a direct dual-typed read spelling such as `s16 signed_val = a2[0]; t0 = (u16)a2[0]; if (signed_val == -2)` (the InitHiraRmd_80041AC8 sibling at text1a.c:1130-1132 achieves the same lh+lhu pair without any alias trick). Function stays INCOMPLETE; a byte-correct cheat cannot be reverted without breaking the oracle, so it remains on main until a clean pure-C redo is produced and Auditor-PASSed.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
